label: Close
description: Sales-focused CRM with built-in calling, email, and SMS — connect via your personal API key.

form.title: Connect Close
form.subtitle: Paste your Close API key to let Anton read and write your CRM data.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste a personal API key from Close Settings → API Keys. Simplest path — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Close API Key

1. Log in to your Close account at [app.close.com](https://app.close.com).
2. Click your avatar or initials in the bottom-left corner, then open **Settings**.
3. Navigate to **Your Profile → API Keys** (or go directly to [app.close.com/profile/api-keys](https://app.close.com/profile/api-keys)).
4. Click **Generate New API Key**.
5. Give it a descriptive name like `Anton`.
6. Copy the key shown — it is only displayed once.
7. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

## Notes

- API keys are scoped to your user account; they inherit your permissions within Close.
- If you need org-wide access, use an admin account to generate the key.
- To revoke access, return to **Settings → API Keys** and delete the key.
- Close uses HTTP Basic Auth under the hood — the key acts as the username with a blank password.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Close at Settings → Your Profile → API Keys. Shown only once at creation time.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Use a Close OAuth 2.0 app you registered in the Close Developer Portal. Suitable for building integrations that access multiple Close organizations.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 — Bring Your Own Client

Use this method if you need token-based access without exposing a raw API key, or if you are building a multi-org integration.

1. Log in at [app.close.com](https://app.close.com) with an admin account.
2. Navigate to **Settings → Integrations → OAuth Apps** (or the Close Developer Portal).
3. Click **Create App** and fill in a name (e.g. `Anton`) and set the redirect URI to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton's local callback).
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown.
5. Paste them into the fields below and click **Submit**.
6. A browser window opens for you (or the end user) to authorize access.

## Notes

- Tokens can be revoked from **Settings → Integrations → OAuth Apps** at any time.
- If you only need personal access to your own account, the **API Key** method above is much simpler.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From the Close Developer Portal → OAuth Apps → your app's Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired client secret from the same OAuth app in the Close Developer Portal.
